asked.question: says sentences with four or more words
says some words from a song or story or nursery rhyme
talks about at least one thing that happended during her day like I played soccer
answers simple questions like what is a coat for or what is crayon for
talks with you in conversation using at least two back and forth exchangess
ask who or what or where or why questions like where is mommy or where is daddy
says what action is happening in a picture or book when asked like running or eating or playing
catches a large ball most of the times
serves herself food or pours water with adult supervision
holds crayon or pencil between fingers and thumb not fist
pretends to be something else during play like teacher or superhero or a dog
asks to go play with children if none are around like can I play with Alex
comforts others who are hurt or sad like hugging a crying friend
avoids danger like not jumping from tall heights at the playgroung
names a few colors or items
tell what comes next in a well known story
draws a person with three or more body parts

speech_language.title: Story Time with Questions
speech_language.instructions: Read a short story together and ask Ari questions about it, prompting her to use sentences.
speech_language.adaptation: Use books with engaging pictures to stimulate discussion.

speech_language.title: Song and Movement
speech_language.why_this_fits: Promotes language through music and physical activity.
speech_language.instructions: Sing a familiar song together, adding simple movements for each verse.
speech_language.adaptation: Choose songs with repetitive phrases to encourage participation.

speech_language.title: Daily Recap
speech_language.why_this_fits: Encourages Ari to express her thoughts and experiences.
speech_language.instructions: At the end of the day, ask Ari to tell you about one thing she did.
speech_language.adaptation: Use visual aids or prompts if needed to help her recall.

speech_language.title: Picture Description
speech_language.instructions: Show Ari a picture and ask her to describe what she sees.
speech_language.materials: A collection of varied pictures (e.g., animals, places).
speech_language.adaptation: Start with simple pictures and gradually introduce more complex scenes.

speech_language.title: Role Play
speech_language.why_this_fits: Fosters imaginative play and language use.
speech_language.instructions: Act out a simple scenario (like shopping or cooking) together.
speech_language.materials: Props related to the scenario (e.g., play food, shopping bags).
speech_language.adaptation: Use familiar scenarios to make it easier for Ari to engage.

motor.title: Ball Toss
motor.why_this_fits: Improves hand-eye coordination and gross motor skills.
motor.instructions: Stand a short distance apart and toss a soft ball back and forth.
motor.adaptation: Start with a larger ball to make catching easier.

motor.title: Obstacle Course
motor.instructions: Set up a simple obstacle course using pillows, chairs, and other safe objects.
motor.adaptation: Adjust the difficulty based on Ari's comfort level.

motor.title: Art with Crayons
motor.why_this_fits: Enhances fine motor skills through creative expression.
motor.instructions: Encourage Ari to draw or color using crayons.
motor.adaptation: Provide thicker crayons for easier grip if needed.

motor.title: Pouring Practice
motor.instructions: Supervise Ari as she pours water from one container to another.
motor.adaptation: Use containers with spouts to make pouring easier.

motor.title: Buttoning Game
motor.why_this_fits: Strengthens fine motor skills and independence.
motor.instructions: Practice buttoning and unbuttoning on a piece of clothing.
motor.materials: A shirt or a fabric with buttons.
motor.adaptation: Use larger buttons for easier manipulation.

social_communication.title: Pretend Play
social_communication.why_this_fits: Encourages imaginative play and social interaction.
social_communication.instructions: Engage in a pretend play scenario, like playing house or store.
social_communication.materials: Props related to the chosen scenario.
social_communication.adaptation: Use familiar roles to make it easier for Ari to participate.

social_communication.title: Emotion Charades
social_communication.why_this_fits: Helps Ari recognize and express emotions.
social_communication.instructions: Take turns acting out different emotions while the other guesses.
social_communication.adaptation: Use simple emotions like happy, sad, and angry.

social_communication.title: Friendship Bracelet Making
social_communication.instructions: Create simple bracelets together using beads.
social_communication.adaptation: Use larger beads for easier handling.

social_communication.title: Compliment Circle
social_communication.instructions: Take turns giving each other compliments.
social_communication.adaptation: Model compliments to guide Ari in expressing her thoughts.

social_communication.title: Turn-Taking Games
social_communication.why_this_fits: Teaches patience and sharing during play.
social_communication.instructions: Play a simple board game that requires taking turns.
social_communication.materials: A board game suitable for her age.
social_communication.adaptation: Choose games with clear rules and visuals.

adaptive_cognitive.title: Color Hunt
adaptive_cognitive.why_this_fits: Enhances color recognition and cognitive skills.
adaptive_cognitive.instructions: Go on a color hunt around the house to find items of a specific color.
adaptive_cognitive.adaptation: Start with primary colors for easier identification.

adaptive_cognitive.title: Story Sequencing
adaptive_cognitive.instructions: Use picture cards to arrange a story in the correct sequence.
adaptive_cognitive.materials: Picture cards from a familiar story.
adaptive_cognitive.adaptation: Use fewer cards for simpler stories.

adaptive_cognitive.title: Matching Game
adaptive_cognitive.instructions: Play a matching game using cards with pictures.
adaptive_cognitive.adaptation: Start with fewer pairs to make it easier.

adaptive_cognitive.title: Puzzle Time
adaptive_cognitive.instructions: Work together to complete a simple jigsaw puzzle.
adaptive_cognitive.materials: A jigsaw puzzle with large pieces.
adaptive_cognitive.adaptation: Choose puzzles with fewer pieces for success.

adaptive_cognitive.title: What Comes Next?
adaptive_cognitive.instructions: Show Ari a familiar story and pause before the end, asking her what she thinks will happen next.
adaptive_cognitive.adaptation: Use stories she enjoys to keep her engaged.

note: CDC milestones only go to 60 months, so older children start from the 60-month band.
